---
title: Glossary
description: One-sentence definitions of the terms used across these docs
order: 9
---

This page collects short definitions and links to the deep reference for each term.

<details>
<summary>**Studyflow**</summary>
A BPMN-based visual language for specifying scientific studies and the data flows around them. See [Why Studyflow](why-studyflow.qmd).
</details>

<details>
<summary>**`.studyflow` file**</summary>
The serialized form of a studyflow diagram: a YAML document (top-level keys `studyflow`, `definitions`, `elements`, `diagram`) semantically equivalent to BPMN 2.0 XML. Legacy XML `.studyflow` files still open, and the modeler can export BPMN 2.0 XML for interop with other BPMN tools.
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Study**</summary>
The top-level element of a diagram, extending `bpmn:Process`. Contains all events, activities, gateways, and data. See [Elements](elements.qmd).
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Element**</summary>
A node in the diagram: an event, activity, gateway, data object, data store, dataset, table, or sub-process.
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Activity**</summary>
A step in the workflow (a rectangle). In Studyflow, specialized as `CognitiveTask`, `Questionnaire`, `Instruction`, `Rest`, `VideoGame`, `Script`, `Manual`, or `BehaverseTask`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Event**</summary>
A trigger point in the workflow (a circle): start, end, intermediate, boundary, timer, or error.
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Gateway**</summary>
A branching/merging point (a diamond): exclusive, parallel, inclusive, complex, event-based, or -- Studyflow-specific -- `RandomGateway` for stochastic branching.
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Sequence flow**</summary>
A directed arrow connecting two elements, specifying execution order.
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Data object / data store**</summary>
BPMN elements for transient and persistent data, respectively. Studyflow refines these with `Dataset`, `Table`, `Array`, `Schema`, and `DataCatalog`. See [Data](data.qmd).
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Data operation**</summary>
A marker on a task indicating a data transformation: `Map`, `Filter`, `Reduce`, `Group`, `FlatMap`, `Transform`, or `Compose`. The task remains a normal BPMN task; the marker adds data semantics. See [Data](data.qmd).
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Choreography**</summary>
A BPMN2 construct for modeling interactions between multiple participants, used in multi-agent studies. See the [Multi-agent collaboration example](../examples/multi-agent.qmd).
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Moddle**</summary>
The schema format used internally by [bpmn-js](https://github.com/bpmn-io) to describe element types and their attributes. Studyflow defines its language as a moddle YAML file.
</details>

<details>
<summary>**View**</summary>
A rendering of a studyflow diagram tuned to a specific need: the *Study view* (BPMN-like overview), the *Timeline view* (Gantt-like), and the *Checklist view*. See [Views](views.qmd).
</details>

<details>
<summary>**BPMN**</summary>
Business Process Model and Notation. The ISO-standard process notation that Studyflow extends. See [Studyflow vs BPMN](studyflow-vs-bpmn.qmd).
</details>

<details>
<summary>**Modeler**</summary>
The web app at [behaverse.org/studyflow-modeler](https://behaverse.org/studyflow-modeler/) used to author and edit `.studyflow` files. See [Modeler app](../guides/modeler-app.qmd).
</details>
